findings.title: Unpinned third-party GitHub Action (pnpm/action-setup@v4)
findings.description: pnpm/action-setup@v4 is referenced by tag not SHA in all workflow files. A compromised pnpm/action-setup repo could inject code that runs in CI. The nightly and manual eval workflows expose ANTHROPIC_API_KEY to this action.
findings.exploit_scenario: 1. Attacker compromises pnpm/action-setup repo or its maintainer account. 2. Attacker force-pushes malicious code to the v4 tag. 3. On next nightly eval run, malicious code executes with access to ANTHROPIC_API_KEY secret. 4. Attacker exfiltrates the API key for unauthorized Anthropic API usage.
findings.impact: Anthropic API key exfiltration, unauthorized API spend, potential CI/CD supply chain compromise.
findings.recommendation: Pin pnpm/action-setup to a specific commit SHA: `uses: pnpm/action-setup@<sha>`. Use Dependabot or Renovate to auto-update the SHA when new versions are released.
